title: key_packages schema: add device_id column with migration
description:
Add device_id TEXT NOT NULL to key_packages table and change PRIMARY KEY to (pub_id, device_id). Add PRAGMA user_version migration infrastructure.

Algorithm:
1. Add migration infrastructure to Store::new() in relay/src/store.rs:
   - After creating tables, read PRAGMA user_version
   - If version < 1: run v0->v1 migration, then PRAGMA user_version = 1
2. v0->v1 migration steps:
   a. CREATE TABLE key_packages_new (pub_id TEXT NOT NULL, device_id TEXT NOT NULL, data BLOB NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')), PRIMARY KEY (pub_id, device_id))
   b. INSERT INTO key_packages_new (pub_id, device_id, data, updated_at) SELECT pub_id, 'legacy', data, updated_at FROM key_packages
   c. DROP TABLE key_packages
   d. ALTER TABLE key_packages_new RENAME TO key_packages
3. Update the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statement at the top to use the new schema (for fresh installs that skip migration)

Defensive rules:
- Migration must run inside a transaction so partial migration is impossible
- CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS at top must match post-migration schema
- Never DROP TABLE without SELECT first (already migrating data via INSERT INTO ... SELECT)
- PRAGMA user_version read/write uses sqlx::query not raw SQL string concat
acceptance_criteria: Store::new() on a fresh DB creates the correct schema; Store::new() on a v0 DB migrates data and sets version=1; cargo test -p nie-relay clean
close_reason: schema migration implemented in Store::new with PRAGMA user_version v0→v1, composite PK (pub_id, device_id)
